movies.name: High Fidelity
movies.review:
Truly a movie about a man lost in life figuring out what it all means.

Fun movie, i think its about John Cusack's character realizing his life's stalled, to the point where life isn't getting bigger anymore, with Laura wishing it did. Figuring out what it all means is really just bro realizing he needs to commit to something. 

I enjoyed the story telling and the main character's style lol.

movies.name: We Live in Time
movies.review: Banger soundtrack

watches.review: Banger soundtrack

Hats off to Bryce Dessner, the music was really well crafted. The score followed the tone of the movie to perfection and cemented the emotional rollercoaster this movie was. The movie felt very tightly crafted, without a lot of extraneous plot lines or misplaced jokes. Every scene, and every time cut felt well placed. 10/10 Can't wait to watch it again 10 years down the line and break down.

movies.name: Night Is Short, Walk On Girl
movies.review: Still as funny the third time, wack ass movie

watches.review: Still as funny the third time, wack ass movie
Time is longer when you’re younger. Maybe this movie is about youth as a whole, the drinking spirit more than the drinking itself

What an absurd, chaotic, weird, and so absolutely weirdly deep-ish movie. We see gods of used books, time, and honestly? characters all representing one of my favorite concepts "the drinking spirit" where its not necessarily about having that one more drink, but more about the spirit of having fun and extending a night that can be afforded to. What a vastly unique movie.

movies.name: Rye Lane
movies.review:
😊 

I see why it’s my comfort movie now 🙈, something about the character arcs and the art style pulling it all together for a pick me up / delightful movie. 

To readers: put in my notice at work this morning, moving back in w the fam at the gaff (Rye Lane reference). Had arguably (recency bias*) my most fun weekend of the decade, met someone really cool, and realized I need to hope my resume may not be too shabby. Open offer to cold call me whenever. I’ma see what happens (Rye Lane reference)

movies.name: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
movies.review:
Movie has the creative chaos of the 2000s that we love to see. Still with a poignant story melded with humor, drama, and pacing that takes a second for the viewer to figure out.

Jim Carrey in a moderately serious role is also very good. There's somewhat of a depth to his character, he says many vile things but somehow it almost feels like the bitterness wasn't a true representation of him but more a symptom of not knowing how to communicate well.

Kate Winslet's character... no notes. Played to perfection and absolutely believable, motivations, actions, and darkness mixed with levity. Peak

watches.review:

Jim Carrey in a moderately serious role is also very good. There's somewhat of a depth to his character, he says many vile things but somehow it almost feels like the bitterness wasn't a true representation of him but more a symptom of not knowing how to communicate well.

movies.name: Sentimental Value
movies.review:
Couldn't wrap my head around the weather at any given point, but beautifully crafted movie. The emphasis of the movie felt more on the relationships and interactions between the characters than a specific character's arc. 

Loved the acting and the cinematography throughout as per usual (I say having seen one other Trier movie).

movies.review:

movies.name: The Life of Chuck
movies.review:
“I am wonderful, I deserve to be wonderful, and I contain multitudes.”

Life-affirming drama sums up this movie so perfectly well, Chuck, the accountant who dances, and yet lives 39 great years, is the subject of a beautiful film encouraging perseverance and the value of so many different perspectives.

movies.name: Death of a Unicorn
movies.review:
In a time of White Lotus and whimsy, Death of a Unicorn goes down the (maybe not the most original) comedic eat the rich path. A lot of the little things in the movie were done great, Sheppy’s fits? Outrageous. Téa Leoni’s performance within a performance? Absurd. NoHo Hank? The foil to it all.

The movie’s fx were definitely a little cheesy but also were very pretty at times and felt very real. As an enjoyer of Bodies, Bodies, Bodies, this was less LMAO but more LOL.

The movie’s fx were definitely a little cheesy but also were very pretty at times and felt very real. As an enjoyer of Bodies, Bodies, Bodies, this was less LMAO but more LOL.

movies.name: Y2K
movies.review:
Peak science fiction. One of the greatest mid-credits scenes known to mankind.

On a realer note, didn’t realize it was a stoner comedy… I loved it but dear god someone wrote this on paper, thought it was a good idea, and managed to get A24 to release and market this nationwide?

10/10 would rewatch (prerequisite might include several beers or other things)

Peak science fiction. One of the greatest mid-credits scenes known to mankind.

On a realer note, didn’t realize it was a stoner comedy… I loved it but dear god someone wrote this on paper, thought it was a good idea, and managed to get A24 to release and market this nationwide?

10/10 would rewatch (prerequisite might include several beers or other things)

movies.name: Flow
movies.review: This movie was beautiful, didn’t shy away from grim realities, and left many mysteries for the viewer to wonder about. No words, no names, but each character had a personality and distinctive characters with the world being Desth Stranding-esque, so much going on that makes nearly no sense. Loved this movie

movies.name: Players
movies.review: Netflix makes a modern rom-com with young people in New York. Cliches, Netflix-signature cinematography and color grading, and mediocre in most ways. Though as a New Girl fan, and a sucker for cliches, I liked it.

watches.review: Thumbs sideways, no excuse to use the foam finger 😭

movies.name: A Real Pain
movies.review:
After a lot of further thought, I really like this movie. The cinematography is great, the overall story really resonates with the main theme of being a tourist in another's pain, and the actors kill their role. 

The thing that left me the most confused when leaving the theater is why they never elaborated on Benji. The rooftop scene I feel like was left on a cliffhanger that would be closer to the real world but in a movie, I would've liked a little bit more clarity. But after a lot of thought, Benji's outbursts were rarely if ever wrong, they may have been very untimely or awkward, but never wrong. Thinking about this from Benji's perspective made the movie make a lot more sense.

Thinking about this from Benji's perspective made the movie make a lot more sense.

movies.name: Look Back
movies.review: Beautiful film not only about the ups and downs creative collaboration but in part about growing up and learning about people in hindsight after your time being close friends. The music was top notch and the voice acting was great. The visuals caught me a little off guard at some points but overall really pretty.

The music was top notch and the voice acting was great. The visuals caught me a little off guard at some points but overall really pretty.

movies.name: Venom: The Last Dance
movies.review:
This movie was fun. Its what I expected from a Venom movie and its a pleasant change of pace from the Sony universe's Morbius (mediocre in all regards, just plain boring) and Madame Web (less than mediocre in all regards, fun in its comparisons of its action to South Indian movies).

Spoiler (really, what to look forward to):

Venom sings Bon Jovi, dances to Dancing Queen, learns about the fun of gambling, and some fun symbiotes. Don't say, "this is stupid" but reframe it as "LMAO" and revel in how unnecessarily (in a fun way, rather this than the movie proper) long these fun tangents go on.

movies.name: Piece By Piece
movies.review: Whimsy documentary that glosses over the dark stuff, but that’s ok. Sounded great, the journey looked beautiful and the Lego medium worked really really well for all but the dark stuff. If whimsy and learning is your thing over absolute realism, this movie is great. The Dogg himself was in it, and so many more collabs that I didn’t know went that deep

The Dogg himself was in it, and so many more collabs that I didn’t know went that deep

movies.name: The Outrun
movies.review: Slow but beautiful movie about addiction. Imo this falls somewhat under the same category of movie as Perfect Days or Aftersun that it’s a sort of slow burn that is visually great and the story deep and meaningful but the pacing does end up being slow yet worth sticking through

watches.review: Slow but beautiful movie about addiction. Imo this falls somewhat under the same category of movie as Perfect Days or Aftersun that it’s a sort of slow burn that is visually great and the story deep and meaningful but the pacing does end up being slow yet worth sticking through

movies.name: The Apprentice
movies.review: Sebastian Stan killed the assignment. This movie covered the birth of a monster and showed how terrible these people were. The movie didn’t bring me joy, make me laugh or be in awe. It did make me cringe, make me angry and other things it probably intended. These stars are mainly just for Sebastian, and I’d probably never watch the movie again, so maybe it did what it wanted to do?

It did make me cringe, make me angry and other things it probably intended. These stars are mainly just for Sebastian, and I’d probably never watch the movie again, so maybe it did what it wanted to do?

movies.name: Saturday Night
movies.review:
When the bongo drums were playing, the tension and the fire felt like we were watching a high pressure continuous 90 minutes that preceded history. But then… the drums stopped. The tension didn’t really break? It kinda just faded until the beats came back moving time along. 

Very fun movie, but I think the pacing has a couple issues that are hard to overlook given the timeline of the movie. Side characters were flushed out, so if you’re a Chevy Chase fan (why?) then maybe you’d like it but I think some of the side stories detracted from the big picture.

movies.name: Maara
movies.review:
The music, the visuals, and the story all together left me in awe and in tears. The movie’s pacing is a little slow towards the end, but the movie hits all the emotional and whimsical beats it needed to, instilling this sort of inspirational good-hearted free-spirited story. 

I’ve been thinking about this movie a lot over the past month, struggling to find its name until I too searched for Maadhavan and uncovered this visually and aurally stunning piece of art.

movies.name: My Old Ass
movies.review: A very beautiful movie, it sounded and looked amazing. It’s a movie all about a central moral of living in the present and not being afraid of your perceptions of the future. Especially being in this stage of life moving away from home and entering the world no longer as a student, this movie hits.

movies.name: Spider-Man: Across the Spider-Verse
movies.review:
Watching this in concert was an amazing experience. The orchestra popped off and added their own beats and music at times augmenting the music's already great score and soundtrack. 

Nothing like cheering with the entire crowd when my boy Pavitar Prabhakar showed up on screen.
